title: Advanced Gay

jokes.text: Thanks... by the way, that square thing is a Hampton bolt and what you just described is a manacheck maneuver.
jokes.setup: Character casually uses technical plumbing terminology
jokes.punchline: The specificity and made-up sound of 'manacheck maneuver'
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Troy's unexpected plumbing knowledge with believable technical jargon

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I just used the manacheck maneuver to fix it.

jokes.text: Unclog one toilet with me and tell me you don't feel something.
jokes.setup: Plumber trying to recruit Troy with passionate appeal
jokes.punchline: Inadvertent romantic/sexual undertones in plumbing recruitment
jokes.explanation: Excellent double entendre that works on multiple levels - passionate mentorship language applied to plumbing creates unintended romantic subtext

dialogue.speaker: Plumber
dialogue.line: Look, Troy, I know you think plumbing is just pipes and toilets, but it's so much more than that. It's a craft. It's a calling. I want you to come work with me. Let me show you the ropes, teach you everything I know. We could be great together.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Uh... I think there's been a misunderstanding here.

dialogue.speaker: Plumber
dialogue.line: No misunderstanding. I see potential in you, Troy. Real potential. Come on, just give me a chance. We'll work nights, weekends, whatever it takes. I'm willing to go deep for this.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: This is making me uncomfortable.

jokes.text: At your cervix.
jokes.setup: Pierce attempting to say 'at your service'
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces it as gynecological term
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce malapropism - perfectly in character but the wordplay itself is fairly basic

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: At your cervix.

jokes.text: Oh, sorry, I thought you were a lady. You can use that cervix line if you want.
jokes.setup: Pierce realizes his mistake
jokes.punchline: Doubles down by offering the inappropriate line to the man
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce logic - instead of being embarrassed, he generously shares his terrible pickup line

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Wait, I didn't mean it that way. Let me rephrase...

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Actually, you know what? That's exactly what I meant. Here, let me say it again even more offensive.

jokes.text: In the wipes business we call them towel-heads.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining wipes business terminology
jokes.punchline: Uses what sounds like a racial slur but means literal towel enthusiasts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - sets up audience expectation of racism but subverts with innocent business terminology

jokes.text: I assumed they were just brothers or wealthy.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining why he didn't recognize they were gay
jokes.punchline: His alternative explanations for their behavior
jokes.explanation: Great Pierce logic connecting unrelated concepts - brothers or wealth as explanations for closeness

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I thought they were just really good friends who happened to live together, share a bed, and were constantly touching each other. You know, like roommates do.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Or maybe they were European. Europeans are very affectionate.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Could've been a medical thing. Some people need to be held to regulate their body temperature.

jokes.text: What do Hawthorne wipes have to do with the choice to be gay?
jokes.setup: Pierce trying to understand the connection
jokes.punchline: Refers to being gay as 'the choice to be gay'
jokes.explanation: Standard Pierce homophobic language disguised as innocent question - in character but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: So let me get this straight... or not straight, I guess. Being gay is a choice?

jokes.text: I'm going to sue the pants off that lady. - I don't think that's a lady. - And why do you want his pants off?
jokes.setup: Pierce uses common expression about suing
jokes.punchline: Jeff points out the sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay on common expression but fairly predictable setup and payoff

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm gonna sue!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's not what 'sue' means.

jokes.text: The only thing I'm attracted to is taking him to court and eating his ass alive. - Ooh! - That's not what I meant.
jokes.setup: Pierce tries to clarify his legal intentions
jokes.punchline: Uses another expression with sexual connotations
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the previous joke - Pierce keeps digging himself deeper with unfortunate word choices

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm not trying to be funny here. I want to make sure my will is airtight.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: When I die, I want everything to go smoothly.

dialogue.speaker: Group
dialogue.line: That's... that's what she said.

jokes.text: Stop putting gay things in my mouth.
jokes.setup: Pierce frustrated by the sexual interpretations
jokes.punchline: His complaint itself has sexual implications
jokes.explanation: Excellent culmination of the running gag - Pierce's frustration expressed in the most unfortunately phrased way possible

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Why does everything have to be about sex? I'm just trying to have a normal conversation here.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I mean, I'm going deep, I'm really going in hard on this topic, and all you people can do is make it dirty.

jokes.text: His life must be hard enough having to sew pouches in all his miniskirts.
jokes.setup: Shirley being sympathetic to drag performer
jokes.punchline: Focuses on practical sewing challenges
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley - well-meaning but hilariously practical focus on the wrong aspect of drag performance

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: You know, I think what you're doing is beautiful. It takes real courage to express yourself like that.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Though I gotta say, honey, those seams are not holding up. You're gonna need a serger and a much better thread tension, or you're gonna have a wardrobe malfunction that's gonna be a lot more revealing than you planned.

jokes.text: They may live in defiance of God, but I'd die before I let a woman touch my hair.
jokes.setup: Shirley's religious disapproval of gay people
jokes.punchline: Admits she needs gay hairstylists despite moral objections
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character hypocrisy - religious condemnation paired with practical dependency on gay services

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I don't believe in the homosexual lifestyle, but I will say this: those gay hairstylists? They are miracles from the Lord.

jokes.text: Hey, what's going on with you bitches? Bitches is gay talk for friends.
jokes.setup: Shirley trying to use gay slang
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly explains her usage
jokes.explanation: Good Shirley awkwardness trying to be progressive but explaining herself defeats the purpose

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: That's so gay!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I mean... happy. I meant it makes me happy. Because gay means happy, right? From the old days?

jokes.text: Pierce's positrons have been negatized, creating anti-Pierce.
jokes.setup: Abed explaining Pierce's behavioral change
jokes.punchline: Uses Inspector Spacetime pseudoscience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed - applying sci-fi logic to character psychology with made-up but believable technobabble

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Pierce's behavior change can only be explained by Inspector Spacetime pseudoscience. He's clearly been exposed to temporal radiation, which is causing him to regress mentally while simultaneously believing he's becoming more enlightened.

jokes.text: Yeah, but anti-inspector had a funny mustache and was kinda rapey.
jokes.setup: Troy engaging with Abed's Inspector Spacetime analogy
jokes.punchline: Points out anti-Inspector's disturbing characteristics
jokes.explanation: Good Troy/Abed dynamic - Troy takes the analogy seriously and adds dark details

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, so the anti-Inspector is like... the evil version?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Exactly. He's everything the Inspector stands against.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: But doesn't he have that thing where he, like, eats people's faces?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's not canonical.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Well, it's disturbing either way.

jokes.text: That's exactly what Constable Reggie said.
jokes.setup: Troy disagreeing with the positron theory
jokes.punchline: Abed references obscure Inspector Spacetime character
jokes.explanation: Deep Abed - doubles down on the fictional universe with specific character reference

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: The positron theory doesn't make any sense.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Actually, it's a fundamental part of Inspector Spacetime lore. Commander Blast established it in the Season 3 episode 'Temporal Mechanics and Breakfast Cereals.'

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I don't remember that episode.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Of course you don't. It featured Commander Blast's third cousin, Ensign Flick, who appeared in exactly one scene with no dialogue.

jokes.text: They may cost more, but they're gayer.
jokes.setup: Pierce describing his new Pride Wipes
jokes.punchline: Treats gayness as a measurable product feature
jokes.explanation: Excellent Pierce business logic - commodifies identity in the most reductive way possible

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: They're Pride Wipes. They clean up gayness.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: One wipe removes up to 50% of the gay.

jokes.text: Greendale's first annual... gay bash.
jokes.setup: Annie reading Pierce's party flyer
jokes.punchline: Unfortunate name that sounds like hate crime
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay on party terminology but fairly obvious joke about the unfortunate naming

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Unfortunate name that sounds like hate crime

jokes.text: If Mexicans were buying his wipes, he would have ridden in on a donkey.
jokes.setup: Jeff criticizing Pierce's motives
jokes.punchline: Specific ridiculous image of Pierce pandering
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff cynicism with vivid absurd imagery - captures Pierce's opportunistic nature perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Pierce, your motives are transparent. You're just pandering to seem relatable.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: That's ridiculous. I'm genuinely trying to connect with the group.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh really? Is that why you showed up to study group in that backwards baseball cap trying to 'rap' about group projects?

jokes.text: In my psych class I'm learning about this thing called the edible complex.
jokes.setup: Britta trying to use psychology terms
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'Oedipal' as 'edible'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta pseudo-intellectual malapropism - tries to sound smart but mangles the terminology

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: That's a real edible complex you've got there.

jokes.text: What's that complex called where you're wrong about everything?
jokes.setup: Jeff responding to Britta's psychology attempt
jokes.punchline: Creates fake psychological condition for being wrong
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jeff comeback - uses Britta's own pseudo-psychology format against her

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I have a condition where I'm allergic to being wrong. It's called Correctness Proximity Syndrome. Very rare. Very serious.

jokes.text: Rainbow, bitches. We can have peanut butter or chocolate chip.
jokes.setup: Pierce enthusiastically suggests rainbow cookies
jokes.punchline: Annie deflates him with limited actual options
jokes.explanation: Good deflation joke, solid character dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: We should get rainbow cookies! You know, with all the colors of the rainbow?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Pierce, there are only three types of cookies at the store: chocolate chip, oatmeal raisin, and sugar cookies.

jokes.text: You get the hell out of here! Go on!
jokes.setup: Troy demonstrates plumbing skill
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sudden aggressive dismissal after praising him
jokes.explanation: Good sudden emotional shift - Jerry's protective instincts kick in seeing Troy's talent

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Wow, that's impressive work, Troy. You really know your way around pipes.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Now get the hell out of my house!

jokes.text: We hate lesbians, we hate baggy pants, and I can't believe you guys are all bears fans.
jokes.setup: Pierce finding commonalities with gay men
jokes.punchline: Confuses 'bears' (gay subculture) with Chicago Bears
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning - Pierce's innocent misunderstanding of gay terminology creates perfect character moment

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Oh, I get it now. You guys and the Chicago Bears fans, you're basically the same thing. You both love bears, you both have your own culture, your own way of dressing...

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I bet you guys go to all the games at Soldier Field too, huh?

jokes.text: Piercinald Anastasia Hawthorne!
jokes.setup: Pierce's father arrives dramatically
jokes.punchline: Pierce's ridiculous full name with feminine middle name
jokes.explanation: Memorable character detail, good setup for father-son dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Dad? What are you doing here?

dialogue.speaker: Cornelius
dialogue.line: I've come to reconcile with you, son.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Really? After all these years?

dialogue.speaker: Cornelius
dialogue.line: Yes. And I want you to know I've always been proud of you, Pierce Hawthorne.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You... you called me by my full name.

dialogue.speaker: Cornelius
dialogue.line: Of course. Pierce Anastasia Hawthorne.

jokes.text: Why is there an astronaut in the corner making paninis?
jokes.setup: Vice Dean explaining secrecy of AC repair school
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of bizarre background element
jokes.explanation: Perfect Community absurdism - throws in completely random visual element as if it's normal

dialogue.speaker: Vice Dean Laybourne
dialogue.line: The AC repair school must remain secret. We can't have everyone knowing about it.

jokes.text: Isn't that right, Black Hitler?
jokes.setup: Vice Dean addressing someone off-screen
jokes.punchline: Refers to person as 'Black Hitler' casually
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd - treats extremely inappropriate nickname as casual workplace reference

dialogue.speaker: Vice Dean Laybourne
dialogue.line: Black Hitler.

jokes.text: Oh, I always assumed your father was dead. Just mathematically.
jokes.setup: Meeting Pierce's elderly father
jokes.punchline: Jeff's matter-of-fact age calculation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jeff - perfectly callous logic delivered matter-of-factly, great economy of language

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So your dad is... how old?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: He's 81.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: And you're 65?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So when you were born, he was 16. Which means he had you when he was 16, which means... he had sex when he was 15.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Jeff!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm just doing the math.

jokes.text: Swedish dogs. Your blood is tainted by generations of race mixing with laplanders. You're basically Finns!
jokes.setup: Cornelius being racist toward Jeff
jokes.punchline: Escalates to extremely specific Nordic racism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating racism - gets more specific and absurd, ending with Finns as ultimate insult

dialogue.speaker: Cornelius
dialogue.line: You know what, Jeff? I don't like you. It's got nothing to do with you being a lawyer or whatever. It's because you're... well, you're not Nordic enough.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What? What does that even mean?

dialogue.speaker: Cornelius
dialogue.line: I'm talking about your cheekbones, your bone structure in general. And don't get me started on your relationship with fermented fish. You probably don't even know how to properly pronounce 'Søren.' Your ancestry is probably diluted with too much Mediterranean. Weak Viking genes. And those shoes? Those are not shoes a true Scandinavian would wear. Your whole presence screams 'non-Nordic sphere of influence.'

jokes.text: Oh, my goodness, he's like the Abed of racism.
jokes.setup: Cornelius displaying detailed racist knowledge
jokes.punchline: Shirley compares his obsessive expertise to Abed's pop culture knowledge
jokes.explanation: Exceptional meta joke - uses established character trait as template for describing extreme racism, perfect economy

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: You know what? That's actually impressive. You've got more racial slurs memorized than Abed's got pop culture references.

jokes.text: I can excuse racism, but I draw the line at animal cruelty. You can excuse racism?
jokes.setup: Shirley stating her moral priorities
jokes.punchline: Annie's horrified reaction to the racism tolerance
jokes.explanation: Perfect moral priority joke - Shirley's backwards values exposed by Annie's logical reaction, became iconic quote

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I can tolerate a lot of things, but racism? That I cannot abide.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're saying you CAN tolerate racism?

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: No, I just said—

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Shirley!

jokes.text: Well, if you like gay people so much, why don't you marry them?!
jokes.setup: Pierce frustrated with Jeff's defense of gay rights
jokes.punchline: Uses playground-level comeback
jokes.explanation: Good Pierce regression to childish argument when frustrated - character-appropriate but basic joke

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Oh, that's great, Jeff. Real progressive. You know what? I'm not homophobic. I have a gay friend. Well, had. He got upset when I told him that being gay was a choice, and that he chose wrong.

jokes.text: This is so edible.
jokes.setup: Britta observing the father-son conflict
jokes.punchline: Still using 'edible' instead of 'Oedipal'
jokes.explanation: Good callback - Britta still hasn't learned the correct term, consistent character flaw

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Still using 'edible' instead of 'Oedipal'

jokes.text: No, that's how he talked to your mom.
jokes.setup: Britta asking if Jeff's dad talked to his mom that way
jokes.punchline: Jeff deflects with 'your mom' joke
jokes.explanation: Standard Jeff deflection using juvenile comeback - in character but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Did your dad talk to your mom that way?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Your mom.

jokes.text: Pierce, Pierce, Pierce!
jokes.setup: Pierce enters his own party
jokes.punchline: Crowd chanting his name like a celebrity
jokes.explanation: Solid visual payoff showing Pierce's unexpected gay icon status

dialogue.speaker: Crowd
dialogue.line: Pierce! Pierce! Pierce!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I feel like a celebrity!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You are a celebrity. You're rich and old.

jokes.text: This is the room! This is the room temperature room.
jokes.setup: Showing Troy the perfect temperature room
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'room temperature'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay concept - the room that defines room temperature, absurd but internally logical

dialogue.speaker: Vice Dean Laybourne
dialogue.line: Troy, welcome to the perfect temperature room. It maintains an ideal 72 degrees at all times.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wow, this is amazing! But wait... isn't this just a regular room?

dialogue.speaker: Vice Dean Laybourne
dialogue.line: Exactly. It's a room. At room temperature. We took the concept literally.

jokes.text: We're an unseen, unknown, unvincible fraternity of craftsmen.
jokes.setup: Laybourne describing AC repairmen
jokes.punchline: Increasingly grandiose alliteration ending with 'unvincible'
jokes.explanation: Good escalating pompousness with made-up word 'unvincible' fitting the character's grandiosity

dialogue.speaker: Vice Dean Laybourne
dialogue.line: They're not just air conditioning repairmen. They're magnificent, marvelous, monumentally masterful mechanics of meteorological maintenance. They're stupendous, stellar, sagacious specialists in systematic temperature control. They're phenomenal, prodigious, positively preeminent paragons of pneumatic perfection. They're absolutely, undeniably, irrefutably unvincible.

jokes.text: Will there always be paninis?
jokes.setup: Troy considering joining AC repair school
jokes.punchline: His main concern is the snack situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy priorities - focuses on the wrong detail in a life-changing decision

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: AC repair school... that's actually pretty practical. But wait, what's the snack situation?

jokes.text: I can't feel my pants.
jokes.setup: Troy leaving the perfect temperature room
jokes.punchline: Sensory disorientation expressed mundanely
jokes.explanation: Good Troy absurdism, perfect childlike observation

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: It's like my eyes are cold but my skin is warm. I don't know where I am anymore.

jokes.text: Cool. I can't feel my pants.
jokes.setup: Troy in perfect temperature room
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of strange physical sensation
jokes.explanation: Troy's innocent observation about unusual sensation

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: This room is at the perfect temperature.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Not too hot, not too cold... it's like Goldilocks designed it.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Although I gotta say, my left foot's been asleep for like twenty minutes.

jokes.text: Look at me now, dad! Yeah, look at me now, dad!
jokes.setup: Pierce dancing at gay party
jokes.punchline: Repeatedly shouting defiance at absent father
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Pierce's daddy issues manifesting publicly

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Dad! Dad, where are you?! I'm dancing! I'm dancing, Dad! You said I'd never amount to anything, but look at me now! I'm at a gay party and I'm DANCING!

jokes.text: Man this party's a real sausage fest.
jokes.setup: Chang commenting on the party
jokes.punchline: Uses straight male complaint at gay male party
jokes.explanation: Decent ironic observation - phrase that's usually negative becomes literally accurate

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Why is everybody staring at me? Oh, I get it. I'm the only straight guy here. Well, joke's on you because I'm not even sure what that means anymore.

jokes.text: I'm gonna eat Spaceman paninis with Black Hitler, and there's nothing you can do about it!
jokes.setup: Troy defending his AC repair school choice
jokes.punchline: Lists the bizarre perks as if they're obviously desirable
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback combination - Troy lists absurd elements as selling points with complete sincerity

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You don't understand. At AC repair school, I get to wear a jumpsuit. A JUMPSUIT. With my name on it. Troy. In cursive.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Plus, we get a 15% discount at Sbarro, and the teacher said I have real potential. He said I'm a natural with my hands.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: And on Fridays, we get to take apart actual air conditioning units and see what's inside. Do you know how cool that is? Literally cool. It's temperature-controlled.

jokes.text: I don't know why the word gay means dumb.
jokes.setup: Pierce reflecting on language
jokes.punchline: Obliviously progressive observation about slur usage
jokes.explanation: Good Pierce accidentally enlightened moment - right conclusion through wrong reasoning

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking about language and how it evolves. Back in my day, we used certain words without thinking twice. But now I realize those words are considered offensive. So I've decided to stop using them... mostly because I can never remember which ones are bad anymore.

jokes.text: They're not--they couldn't be more or less identical.
jokes.setup: Abed suggesting plumbing and AC repair are similar
jokes.punchline: Troy's passionate defense gets grammatically confused
jokes.explanation: Good Troy confusion, shows his emotional investment

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You know, plumbing and AC repair are basically the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: No they're not! One is pipes and one is... air conditioning! They're completely different things that require different skills and different tools and different... different knowledge!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: But they both involve—

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Don't you dare finish that sentence! What you're saying is illogical and also stupid!

jokes.text: was a huge mistake.
jokes.setup: Pierce starting what sounds like a positive speech
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's denouncing the whole event
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up expectation of gratitude then completely reverses

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I want to say something. You know, I've been part of this group for a while now, and I've seen a lot of things. A lot of growth. A lot of... togetherness. And that's why I have to be honest with you all.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: This whole thing is terrible. This event, this group, this... everything. I'm denouncing all of it.

jokes.text: Fake heart attack. I taught him everything he knows.
jokes.setup: Cornelius appears to have heart attack
jokes.punchline: Pierce casually reveals it's fake and takes credit
jokes.explanation: Great Pierce moment - proud of teaching manipulation tactics, treats serious medical emergency casually

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Psych! I'm fine. I was just seeing if you all actually cared about me. Turns out you do. Well, except for you, Britta.

jokes.text: Listen up, colonel crypt-keeper.
jokes.setup: Jeff addressing Cornelius
jokes.punchline: Creative insult combining military rank with horror reference
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff insult - combines authority reference with appearance-based horror movie allusion

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You're like a Major pain in my Nightmare on Elm Street.

jokes.text: So what?
jokes.setup: Jeff's passionate speech about bad fathers
jokes.punchline: Dismisses it all with simple phrase
jokes.explanation: Brilliant climax - builds elaborate argument then deflates it with perfect dismissive phrase. Maximum economy.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what? I don't care anymore. My father was a bad man, and I'm not going to waste my time being angry about it. Life's too short.

jokes.text: Did you know that the Canadian Mallard is the only water fowl that bites his father's head off on the way out of the womb?
jokes.setup: Pierce sharing nature fact
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up animal behavior that relates to his situation
jokes.explanation: Great Pierce pseudo-intellectual moment - invents specific animal fact that conveniently parallels his father issues

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Did you know that the African tree frog can change its gender based on environmental stress? I read that somewhere. Probably National Geographic or the back of a cereal box.

jokes.text: I looked it up on the 'wackapeediah'
jokes.setup: Pierce defending his made-up fact
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces Wikipedia in childish way
jokes.explanation: Standard Pierce malapropism - makes Wikipedia sound like silly children's site

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: It's on Wikipedia!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Wickerpedia!

jokes.text: I looked it up on the 'wackapeediah'
jokes.setup: Pierce claiming to have researched his animal fact
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces Wikipedia as 'wackapeediah'
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce malapropism, perfect character voice

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I did my research on Wackapeediah.

jokes.text: He bought a lot of his organs from questionable sources.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining father's death
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to black market organ trading
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - treats horrific medical practices as casual family history

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: My father died under mysterious circumstances. The official report said organ failure, but I've always suspected the black market was involved. Either way, I got two of his kidneys in the will.

jokes.text: But the terms of his will clearly state that whoever kills him gets to keep it.
jokes.setup: Pierce offering Jeff his inheritance
jokes.punchline: Will supposedly rewards murder
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark absurdist logic - legal document that incentivizes patricide, perfectly deadpan delivery

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Jeff, I'm leaving you something in my will.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Pierce, that's very generous, but you're not going anywhere.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I know, but I want you to have it anyway. It's my inheritance.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, the will says if you murder me, you get everything.

jokes.text: 'Well, I'm not scared of you anymore because you're dead, and I'm not. So, I win.' And you can suck it.
jokes.setup: Pierce reading eulogy
jokes.punchline: Childish victory declaration followed by crude addendum
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce eulogy - reduces death to competition then adds crude insult for emphasis

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm going to live forever!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Also, suck it, death.

jokes.text: So you're going to be what? A plumber? No, I'm not gonna be a plumber either. Because they have to deal with poop.
jokes.setup: Laybourne assuming Troy will choose plumbing
jokes.punchline: Troy rejects both careers for childish reason
jokes.explanation: Good Troy moment - reduces career decision to basic bodily function concern

dialogue.speaker: Vice Dean Laybourne
dialogue.line: So Troy, you've narrowed it down between air conditioning repair and plumbing?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Actually, I've decided to reject both of those paths.

dialogue.speaker: Vice Dean Laybourne
dialogue.line: Oh? And why is that?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Because they both sound like poop jobs.

jokes.text: Hey, Winger. You going home alone? Gay.
jokes.setup: Episode about tolerance and gay rights
jokes.punchline: Chang immediately uses 'gay' as insult
jokes.explanation: Good ironic ending, perfect Chang obliviousness to episode's themes

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: That's so gay.

jokes.text: How old are you guys? The question isn't how old we are... But when old we are!
jokes.setup: Annie asking reasonable question about their behavior
jokes.punchline: Troy/Abed respond with meaningless Inspector Spacetime catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good tag showing Troy/Abed back to their normal dynamic with nonsensical sci-fi response

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: What are you two doing?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Shut up and sit down!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Shut up and sit down!

jokes.text: The question isn't how old we are... But when old we are!
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed playing Inspector Spacetime
jokes.punchline: Troy attempts Abed-style sci-fi dialogue but mangles it
jokes.explanation: Good Troy/Abed dynamic, shows Troy trying to match Abed's style

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: The temporal mechanics of this paradox require us to utilize the quantum entanglement of—wait, I'm doing it wrong, aren't I?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Yeah, you're doing it very wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I was trying to sound like you!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I know. That's why it was wrong.

tmdb_overview: When a drag queen's song makes Hawthorne Wipes an iconic gay product, Pierce embraces it, until his homophobic dad shows up to squelch the idea.
insight: Character-driven absurdism sustains 82-point episode with steady 2.5 jokes per minute.
